page: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration includes 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-capacity local storage directly bundled with the instance. The AMD EPYC Genoa processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) for vector and neural network acceleration, alongside cryptographic extensions such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong compute capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 3144.58 places it in the top 10% tier, while the multi-core stress-ng score of 100562.57 ranks in the top 25%. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 8.0 GB per core combined with a substantial 3.2 TB NVMe SSD offers a balanced resource profile for memory-intensive workloads without requiring dedicated, high-cost bare-metal hardware.

Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the large local NVMe storage, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data processing, caching layers, and enterprise applications. The inclusion of AVX-512 instructions also makes it capable of handling vector calculations and moderate machine learning inference workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: The Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-3200s-amd is an x86_64 server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It features 32 physical cores (32 vCPUs) running at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark testing, it delivers top-tier single-core performance (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance (top 25%). The processor supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and AES. This configuration is highly suitable for memory-intensive databases, data processing, and caching workloads that benefit from large memory capacity and fast local storage.
